ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON JUN-16-2011 IN 220, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER MAINTAINING CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE BY SEVERING CASE FROM GROUP AND PROMPTLY ASSIGNING OUT FOR TRIAL. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER MAINTAINING CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE BY SEVERING CASE FROM GROUP AND PROMPTLY ASSIGNING OUT FOR TRIAL IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED. THE REQUESTED 'PREFERENCE' TRIAL DATE IS MORE THAN 120 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION. EVEN ASSUMING THE CASE WAS GIVEN PREFERENCE STATUS, THE REQUEST TO 'PROMPTLY' ASSIGN THE CASE OUT FOR TRIAL IS SUBJECT TO THE DETERMINATION WHETHER, ON THE TRIAL DATE, THE PLAINTIFF EXHAUSTED ALL SETTLEMENT EFFORTS AND WAS COMMITTED TO GO TO VERDICT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (220)
